name: Shelkopolis
identity: Largest metropolis of Shelk and one of the major urban engines of the Principalities, defined by fashion, trade, refined civic order, and visible devotional plurality.
approach: Pale stone walls rise from meadows with golden accents, indigo and emerald banners, and flower-lined roads watched by bright-plumed Roadwardens.
inside: Curving smooth streets link elegant upper quarters, busy workshop districts, and purposeful lower wards. Textiles, tools, shrines, taverns, and scholars give the city constant motion.
who_is_out_of_place: those ignoring decorum at noble sites
careless magic users near protected institutions
expected_behavior: respect local fashion and craft traditions
follow Roadwarden road and market order
treat shrines and archives with reverence
visible_power: clerical institutions of Cysur, Cyfoes, Felujitas, and Felelem
faith: Compassion, contemplation, renewal, and fear each have visible public spaces and offerings.
seasonal_change: Balanced seasonal weather preserves outdoor markets and floral ornamentation; observance bands intensify noble and artisan gatherings.
law_context: Fashion and decorum law intensify in noble and ceremonial spaces.
Intellectual property and espionage law are serious in elite and archival sites.
public_behavior_texture: People who belong here are usually artisans, merchants, nobles, and public conduct is read through expectations such as observe site-specific decorum, respect local fashion and craft traditions.
visible_power_signals: Ordinary decisions are visibly shaped by House Shelk nobility, Roadwardens Order, Fashion Artisans Collective.
local_tension_surface: The place feels stable only until pressures such as glyph corruption beyond the city, trade security obligations, noble rivalry begin showing through daily routines.
differentiator: Shelkopolis stands apart because its economy leans on fashion production, enchanted tailoring; public faith remains visible through Compassion, contemplation, renewal, and fear each have visible public spaces and offerings.; outsiders are noticed fastest when they resemble those ignoring decorum at noble sites, open saboteurs.
encounter_rhythm: Most first scenes in Shelkopolis begin through city rhythms: arrival, observation, minor correction, and only then deeper access.
